title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: cam_*() family functions can used implement time--front--camera (TIFC) pipeline estimating wildlife density individual camera locations1. method calculates much total time species spends camera’s field view, converts area-standardised density estimate (individuals/km²) using camera’s effective detection distance (EDD) number days operational. functions designed work reports (.e., output) WildTrax, can read user’s R environment either csv file using data download functions available wildrtrax R package.

title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: cam_*() functions depend several internal lookup tables stored package’s sysdata.rda: loaded automatically package need supplied user.
code: # Load packages library(wildrtrax) library(tidyverse) library(sciCentRverse)

title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: functions workflow expect output WildTrax. Data can downloaded using wildrtrax package. example downloads main image reports ABMI’s Biodiversity Trajectories 2023 project using wt_download_report() function wildrtrax. Extract tidy image main reports separately:
code: # Authenticate into WildTrax wt_auth()  # Find project of interest bdt_proj <- wt_get_projects(sensor = "CAM") |>   filter(project == "Biodiversity Trajectories 2023") |>   select(project, project_id)  bdt_proj_id <- bdt_proj$project_id  # Download reports for all projects ls_bdt_reports <- wt_download_report(   project_id = bdt_proj_id,   sensor_id = "CAM",   reports = c("main", "image_report") ) # Image report: one row per image df_image_report <- ls_bdt_reports |>   (\(x) x[str_detect(names(x), "image_report\\.csv$")])() |>   list_rbind() |>   left_join(bdt_proj, by = "project_id")  # Main report: one row per image × species tag df_main_report <- ls_bdt_reports |>   (\(x) x[str_detect(names(x), "main_report\\.csv$")])() |>   list_rbind() |>   left_join(bdt_proj, by = "project_id")

title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: cam_get_op_days() builds per-day operational calendar camera image report dataframe. day counted operational least one -range image exists (.e. image_fov != "OOR"). Images triggered "CodeLoc Entered" excluded entirely. result long tibble one row per camera per day, operating logical column.
code: df_days <- df_image_report |>   cam_get_op_days(     grouping   = c("project_id", "project", "location_id", "location"),     missing_as = TRUE,     span       = "data"   ) head(df_days, 10)

title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: cam_summarise_op_by_season() aggregates daily calendar built previous step seasonal operating-day totals. Seasons defined Julian day start cutoffs. example, standard ABMI season definitions Julian days 99, 143, 288, correspond beginning spring, summer, winter periods downstream EDD calculations. wide output one row per camera location columns spring, summer, winter, total_days, counts operational days.
code: # Standard ABMI season definitions seasons <- c(spring = 99L,   # ~April 9              summer = 143L,  # ~May 23              winter = 288L)  # ~October 15  df_days_summary <- df_days |>   cam_summarise_op_by_season(     seasons  = seasons,     by_year  = FALSE,     wide     = TRUE   # one column per season + total_days   ) head(df_days_summary, 10)

title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: WildTrax stores tags image × individual tag level: two deer visible one image different tag categories (e.g., one adult one juvenile), two rows main report. cam_consolidate_tags() collapses one row per image × species, summing counts concatenating age/sex classes, needed downstream calculations. function also detects warns likely duplicate tag entries (identical rows except tag_id) drops unknown species_common_name labels warning — indicate upstream data quality issues worth investigating.
code: df_main_report_cons <- cam_consolidate_tags(df_main_report)

previous_headings: Step 4: Time in front of camera
title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: cam_calc_time_by_series() groups consecutive images species camera series using time-gap threshold. Images split_gap_secs seconds apart (default 120 s) start new series. series assigned duration using time--front--camera (TIFC) method, several adjustments account probabilities animal left camera field view: NONE-bridged gaps: cam_obtain_n_gap_class() called internally detect cases animal briefly leaves field view — NONE image triggered — returns within gap threshold. Without check, pair collapsed single series -estimate time spent. detected boundary forces new series regardless time gap. Probabilistic gap adjustment: within-series gaps 20–120 s fall ambiguous zone — short confidently split new series, long enough animal may temporarily left field view. default (adjust_gap_prob = TRUE), species-group-specific probability departure applied sides gaps, reducing time credited across proportionally. Set adjust_gap_prob = FALSE disable compare results assumptions. output one row per series columns project, location, species_common_name, series_num, n_images, series_total_time (seconds), series_start, series_end.
code: df_series <- cam_calc_time_by_series(df_main_report_cons) head(df_series, 10)

previous_headings: Step 4: Time in front of camera
title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: cam_sum_total_time() rolls series-level times seasonal totals per camera × species, zero-filling locations detections species thespecies_universe argument. output one row per camera × species × season total_duration (seconds TIFC) total_season_days (operational days season).
code: sp_uni <- c("White-tailed Deer", "Black Bear", "Moose",             "Woodland Caribou", "Fisher", "Marten",             "Coyote", "Snowshoe Hare", "Canada Lynx")  df_dur <- df_series |>   cam_sum_total_time(     season_cutoffs   = seasons,     op_days_df       = df_days_summary,     species_universe = sp_uni   ) head(df_dur, 10)

title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: EDD values vary camera model. cam_extract_model_lookup() derives model label ("hf2" "pc900") camera equipment_modelfield image report.
code: df_model <- df_image_report |>   cam_extract_model_lookup(     keys      = c("project", "project_id", "location", "location_id"),     model_col = "equipment_model"   )

title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: cam_calc_density_by_loc() joins EDD values internal lookup table camera × species × season row applies TIFC density formula. EDD lookups keyed species distance group, vegetation category (overall_category), season, camera model, mounting height. Users can adjust camera angle height, format output (long wide), choose whether aggregate density value location across seasons sampling. output dataframe reports density location number individuals per km². Note users must supply lookup table location’s EDD vegetation category (.e., edd_category_df), well indicate height camera.
code: df_density <- df_dur |>   left_join(df_model, by = c("project", "location")) |>   mutate(height = "high") |>              # 1 m mounting height   cam_calc_density_by_loc(     edd_category_df     = df_bdt_edd,     # vegetation category per location     cam_fov_angle       = 40,             # degrees     format              = "long",     aggregate           = TRUE,           # weighted mean across seasons     use_global_edd      = TRUE,           # fall back to pooled EDD if needed     annotate_edd_source = TRUE            # label EDD provenance   )  head(df_density, 10)

title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
text: default, Black Bear detections winter excluded seasonal weighted mean aggregating (bears hibernating zero detections reflect low density). controlled agg_exclude_species agg_exclude_season:
code: # Default: exclude Bear × winter cam_calc_density_by_loc(   ...,   agg_exclude_species = "Bear",   agg_exclude_season  = "winter" )  # To include all seasons: cam_calc_density_by_loc(   ...,   agg_exclude_species = NULL )

title: Estimating mammal density using the cam_*() workflow
code: # 1. Operating days df_days <- df_image_reports |>   cam_get_op_days(grouping = c("project_id", "project", "location_id", "location"),                   span = "data", missing_as = TRUE)  df_days_summary <- df_days |>   cam_summarise_op_by_season(seasons = c(spring = 99L, summer = 143L, winter = 288L),                              wide = TRUE)  # 2. Consolidate tags df_main_reports_cons <- cam_consolidate_tags(df_main_reports)  # 3. Time in front of camera df_series <- df_main_reports_cons |>   cam_calc_time_by_series(split_gap_secs = 120)  df_dur <- df_series |>   cam_sum_total_time(season_cutoffs   = c(spring = 99L, summer = 143L, winter = 288L),                      op_days_df       = df_days_summary,                      species_universe = sp_uni)  # 4. Density df_model <- df_image_reports |>   cam_extract_model_lookup(keys = c("project", "location"))  df_density <- df_dur |>   left_join(df_model, by = c("project", "location")) |>   mutate(height = "high") |>   cam_calc_density_by_loc(edd_category_df     = df_bdt_edd,                           cam_fov_angle       = 40,                           aggregate           = TRUE,                           use_global_edd      = TRUE,                           annotate_edd_source = TRUE)

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: [!IMPORTANT] package intended internal use within ABMI Science Centre serves development playground prototyping, testing, iterating cross-project functions workflows.

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: can install package directly GitHub:
code: devtools::install_github("ABbiodiversity/sciCentRverse")

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: package includes following utility functions:

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: add_alberta_flag() - Add Alberta flag site data frame using provincial boundaries extract_by_year() - Extract raster values year spatial features parallel_extract_directory() - Parallel extraction raster data stored directory

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: estimate_processing_time() - Estimate total processing time based subset format_time_diff() - Format time difference days, hours, minutes, seconds

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: load_most_recent_data() - Load recent .RData file dynamically load_rdata_files() - Load .RData files list read_tifs_to_list() - Read TIF files named list read_tifs_to_multiband() - Read TIF files multiband raster object

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: select_one_response() - Select single response column data select_species() - Select specific species column sample_blocks() - Sample fraction rows within blocks snake_case() - Convert strings snake_case summarize_column_classes() - Generate data frame column classes

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: set_terra_options() - Set Terra options buffer memory management

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: theme_science() - Minimal theme scientific plots theme_science_map() - Minimal theme scientific map plots style_active_file() - Style active R file using styler

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: cam_get_op_days() - Get dataframe operational days camera cam_summarise_op_by_season() - Summarise operational days user-defined seasons cam_consolidate_tags() - Consolidate per-image species tags one row WildTrax main report cam_obtain_n_gap_class() - Identify “N” gap boundaries NONE found animal images cam_calc_time_by_series() - Calculate time--front--camera series (seconds) cam_sum_total_time() - Summarise total time project, location, species, season (op-days) cam_extract_model_lookup() - Extract camera models image report cam_calc_density_by_loc() - Calculate density location seasonal time EDD

title: Personal R Utility Functions
code: library(sciCentRverse)  # Example: Convert strings to snake_case snake_case(c("Normal 1991_2020 AHM", "Normal 1991_2020 bFFP"))  # Example: Format time difference start_time <- Sys.time() Sys.sleep(5) end_time <- Sys.time() format_time_diff(start_time, end_time)  # Example: Use custom ggplot2 theme library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point() +   labs(title = "Example Plot", x = "Weight", y = "MPG") +   theme_science()

title: Personal R Utility Functions
text: package requires following R packages: - dplyr - sf - terra - rnaturalearth - ggplot2 - glue - parallel

title: Add Alberta flag to a site data frame using precise boundaries — add_alberta_flag
text: dat Data frame latitude longitude columns lat_col Name latitude column (default: "latitude") lon_col Name longitude column (default: "longitude")

title: Add Alberta flag to a site data frame using precise boundaries — add_alberta_flag
text: Data frame in_alberta column (TRUE/FALSE logical)

what: Calculate density at each location from seasonal time and EDD (with optional pooled fallback) — cam_calc_density_by_loc
title: Calculate density at each location from seasonal time and EDD (with optional pooled fallback) — cam_calc_density_by_loc
text: Computes seasonal (aggregated) density estimates per deployment × species using time--front--camera (TIFC) totals effective detection distance (EDD) lookups. Supports exact EDD matches species group, vegetation category, season, —available—camera model height. Optionally applies dist-group–specific pooled fallback exact EDDs missing. Note filtering locations total days certain threshold (e.g., 30 days) done prior running function.

title: Calculate density at each location from seasonal time and EDD (with optional pooled fallback) — cam_calc_density_by_loc
code: cam_calc_density_by_loc(   duration_df,   edd_category_df,   model_df = NULL,   cam_fov_angle = 40,   format = c("long", "wide"),   include_project = TRUE,   height_col = "height",   dist_groups_df = NULL,   edd_df = NULL,   aggregate = FALSE,   agg_exclude_species = "Bear",   agg_exclude_season = "winter",   use_global_edd = FALSE,   annotate_edd_source = TRUE )

title: Calculate density at each location from seasonal time and EDD (with optional pooled fallback) — cam_calc_density_by_loc
text: duration_df Tibble : project, location, species_common_name, season, total_season_days, total_duration (seconds). present, model /height used directly. edd_category_df Tibble vegetation/EDD category per camera; must contain overall_category plus (project,location) project_location. model_df Optional tibble (project,location,model) attach duration_df lacks model column. cam_fov_angle Camera FOV degrees. Default 40. format "long" (default) "wide". include_project Keep project wide output. Default TRUE. height_col Column name duration_df use height (default "height"). dist_groups_df Optional override mapping (species_common_name -> dist_group). NULL, object named dist_groups must exist env/package. edd_df Optional override EDD table. NULL, object named edd must exist. Must include: dist_group, season, overall_category, edd; ideally n, optionally model, height. aggregate Logical; TRUE, return weighted mean per deployment × species (weights = total_season_days). Rows matching agg_exclude_species × agg_exclude_season removed aggregation. See also agg_exclude_species. agg_exclude_species Regex pattern (case-insensitive) species exclude aggregation specific season. Default "Bear". Set NULL disable exclusion entirely. agg_exclude_season Season label(s) exclude matching species. Default "winter". Set NULL disable exclusion entirely. use_global_edd Logical; TRUE, fill missing exact EDDs using dist-group–specific plan. annotate_edd_source Logical; TRUE (default), add edd_source column values "observed", "prefilled", "pooled". See Details.

title: Calculate density at each location from seasonal time and EDD (with optional pooled fallback) — cam_calc_density_by_loc
text: Seasonal densities (long/wide) weighted aggregate (aggregate = TRUE).

title: Calculate density at each location from seasonal time and EDD (with optional pooled fallback) — cam_calc_density_by_loc
text: Inputs joins duration_df must contain: project, location, species_common_name, season, total_season_days (days operation season), total_duration (seconds TIFC season). present, model /height used directly. edd_category_df must provide overall_category (project, location) (via project_location gets split). Species mapped EDD groups via dist_groups (override dist_groups_df). EDDs taken edd (override edd_df). Exact joins use keys: dist_group, overall_category, season, , present data lookup, model /height. multiple EDD rows share keys, entry largest n chosen. Density calculation Area per season (m²): area_m2 = π * (edd^2) * (cam_fov_angle / 360). Effort per season (m²·10⁻²): effort = total_season_days * (area_m2 / 100) (legacy factor). CPUE: cpue = total_duration / effort. Density (km⁻²): density_km2 = (cpue / 86400) * 10000. edd, effort, total_season_days missing/zero, density_km2 NA. Pooled EDD fallback (use_global_edd = TRUE) Uses weighted pooled EDDs (weights = n) following conservative plan per dist_group: Coyote, Deer, LargeUngulates: pool overall_category (keys: dist_group, season, model, height; absent keys ignored). Bear, Hare, Lynx, SmallMustelids, Wolf: try pooled-overall first, pool height. pooling supplies EDD annotate_edd_source = TRUE, marks edd_source = "pooled". pooling fails, density remains NA. EDD source annotation (annotate_edd_source = TRUE) Adds edd_source column three levels: "observed": EDD came direct match lookup table n > 0 (real measured detections). "prefilled": EDD came direct match n = 0, meaning value pre-filled sysdata EDD table cover missing model/height combination within vegetation category. "pooled": exact match existed; EDD derived runtime use_global_edd pooling across vegetation categories. Aggregation (aggregate = TRUE) Returns one row per deployment × species weighted.mean(density_km2, w = total_season_days, na.rm = TRUE). Removes Bear–winter rows aggregation (including zeros). annotate_edd_source = TRUE, edd_source carried using conservative level across seasons: "pooled" > "prefilled" > "observed". Outputs format = "long": per-season rows overall_category, model, height, total_season_days, total_duration, density_km2, (optionally) edd_source. format = "wide": densities pivoted one row per deployment (diagnostic season-day columns included). aggregate = TRUE: one row per deployment × species (seasons).

title: Calculate density at each location from seasonal time and EDD (with optional pooled fallback) — cam_calc_density_by_loc
text: Marcus Becker

title: Calculate density at each location from seasonal time and EDD (with optional pooled fallback) — cam_calc_density_by_loc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # dur is the output of cam_sum_total_time(), with model and height columns added density <- dur |>   dplyr::left_join(model_lookup, by = c("project", "location")) |>   dplyr::mutate(height = "high") |>   dplyr::filter(total_season_days >= 30) |>   cam_calc_density_by_loc(     edd_category_df     = edd_categories,     cam_fov_angle       = 40,     format              = "long",     aggregate           = TRUE,     use_global_edd      = TRUE,     annotate_edd_source = TRUE   ) } # }

what: Calculate time-in-front-of-camera by series (in seconds) — cam_calc_time_by_series
title: Calculate time-in-front-of-camera by series (in seconds) — cam_calc_time_by_series
text: Groups consecutive images per-species "series" deployment using time-gap threshold, converts image timestamps time--front--camera (TIFC) seconds. Bookend images series get extra tbp/2 account shutter cadence; interior images receive half time previous next.

title: Calculate time-in-front-of-camera by series (in seconds) — cam_calc_time_by_series
code: cam_calc_time_by_series(   cons_main_report,   split_gap_secs = 120,   tbp_lookup = NULL,   n_gap_df = NULL,   adjust_gap_prob = TRUE )

title: Calculate time-in-front-of-camera by series (in seconds) — cam_calc_time_by_series
text: cons_main_report Data frame (cam_consolidate_tags()) least: project, location, image_id, image_date_time (POSIXct), species_common_name, individual_count split_gap_secs Gap threshold start new series (seconds). Default 120. tbp_lookup Optional tibble per-species time--photos tbp (seconds). Columns required: species_common_name, tbp. NULL, function try use internal package object tbi (R/sysdata.rda). available, falls back tbp = 6 seconds species warns. n_gap_df Optional output cam_obtain_n_gap_class(). supplied, image flagged N-gap boundary (animal image immediately followed NONE block next -species image) forces new series start image , regardless time gap. prevents NONE-bridged pair counted single continuous series. Matched image_id × species_common_name. adjust_gap_prob Logical. TRUE (default), applies species-specific probability--leaving-FOV correction within-series gaps 20–120 s, using internal gap_groups leave_prob_pred lookup tables. Set FALSE skip adjustment treat within-series gaps fully occupied — example, compare results assumptions working primarily species covered gap-group lookup.

title: Calculate time-in-front-of-camera by series (in seconds) — cam_calc_time_by_series
text: Tibble one row per series species: project, location, species_common_name, series_num, n_images, series_total_time (seconds), series_start, series_end

title: Calculate time-in-front-of-camera by series (in seconds) — cam_calc_time_by_series
text: Inputs & filtering Excludes rows species_common_name c("STAFF/SETUP","NONE") individual_count == "VNA". image_date_time must POSIXct; function stops . Rows de-duplicated (project,location,image_id,species_common_name, image_date_time,individual_count) avoid double-counting. Series definition Within project × location × species_common_name, rows sorted image_date_time (image_id) previous-gap (seconds) computed. new series starts first image gap exceeds split_gap_secs (default 120 s). series_num cumulative count starts. Per-image time allocation image, diff_prev_s elapsed time since previous image series; diff_next_s time next image series. Bookends get zeros missing side. Interior image time: (diff_prev_s + diff_next_s)/2. Bookend image time: ((diff_prev_s + diff_next_s)/2) + (tbp/2). Image time multiplied individual_count (coerced numeric) obtain image_time_ni_s. Note: single-image series receives tbp/2 (tbp) unless choose post-adjust downstream. Probabilistic gap adjustment (adjust_gap_prob = TRUE) Within-series gaps 20–120 s fall ambiguous zone: gap short confidently split new series, long enough animal may temporarily left camera's field view (FOV). account , species-group-specific probability FOV departure (pred) looked internal leave_prob_pred table (derived empirical gap-length models) applied sides gap: $$diff\_prev\_adj = diff\_prev \times (1 - pred)$$ $$diff\_next\_adj = diff\_next \times (1 - pred)$$ reduces time credited across gap proportionally likely animal absent. gap exactly 20 s receives small reduction; gap near 120 s receives much larger one. Species assigned gap groups via internal gap_groups lookup. species present lookup (e.g., uncommonly detected taxa) receives adjustment — within-series gaps treated though animal continuously present. concerns given analysis, set adjust_gap_prob = FALSE disable adjustment entirely inspect results assumptions. Time--photos (tbp) Supply tbp_lookup columns species_common_name, tbp (seconds), let function try use internal package object tbi (R/sysdata.rda). neither available, warning issued tbp = 6 seconds used fallback. tbp_lookup uses different tbp column name (e.g., time_between_photos, tbi, tbp_seconds), re-mapped tbp.

title: Calculate time-in-front-of-camera by series (in seconds) — cam_calc_time_by_series
text: Marcus Becker

title: Calculate time-in-front-of-camera by series (in seconds) — cam_calc_time_by_series
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # cons_report is the output of cam_consolidate_tags()  # Standard usage — probabilistic gap adjustment applied by default series <- cam_calc_time_by_series(cons_report)  # With N-gap boundaries to split NONE-bridged series n_gaps <- cam_obtain_n_gap_class(cons_report) series <- cam_calc_time_by_series(cons_report, n_gap_df = n_gaps)  # Disable probabilistic adjustment to compare results under both assumptions series_unadj <- cam_calc_time_by_series(cons_report, adjust_gap_prob = FALSE) } # }

what: Consolidate per-image species tags into one row from a WildTrax main report — cam_consolidate_tags
title: Consolidate per-image species tags into one row from a WildTrax main report — cam_consolidate_tags
text: (project_id, location, location_id, image_id, species_common_name) group, sum individual_count (numeric rows) collapse age_class / sex_class replicating tag row's count joining ", ". image_date_time retained must identical within image. Non-species rows (e.g., "STAFF/SETUP", "NONE") preserved unchanged. Rows whose species_common_name neither native_sp non-species list dropped warning listing unexpected labels row counts.

title: Consolidate per-image species tags into one row from a WildTrax main report — cam_consolidate_tags
code: cam_consolidate_tags(report)

title: Consolidate per-image species tags into one row from a WildTrax main report — cam_consolidate_tags
text: report data.frame/tibble least: project_id, location, location_id, image_id, image_date_time, species_common_name, individual_count, age_class, sex_class, tag_id. main report WildTrax.

title: Consolidate per-image species tags into one row from a WildTrax main report — cam_consolidate_tags
text: tibble one row per image × species: project_id, location, location_id, image_id, image_date_time, species_common_name, age_class, sex_class, individual_count (character).

title: Consolidate per-image species tags into one row from a WildTrax main report — cam_consolidate_tags
text: Species identification uses internal character vector native_sp saved R/sysdata.rda. function also detects, warns , removes likely duplicate tag entries fields identical except tag_id, possible double-entry flag. duplicate check applied species rows , using fields: project_id, location, location_id, image_id, image_date_time, species_common_name, individual_count, age_class, sex_class. duplicates found, function keeps one row per identical set (one lowest tag_id) drops rest.

title: Consolidate per-image species tags into one row from a WildTrax main report — cam_consolidate_tags
text: Marcus Becker

title: Consolidate per-image species tags into one row from a WildTrax main report — cam_consolidate_tags
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ x <- cam_consolidate_tags(main_reports) # If duplicate tags were removed, you'll see a warning listing image_id(s). } # }

title: Extract per-camera model (
text: Minimal helper derive per-camera model model column (defaults equipment_model). Returns one row per camera key model lower case.

title: Extract per-camera model (
code: cam_extract_model_lookup(   image_report,   keys = c("project", "location"),   model_col = "equipment_model",   hf2_pattern = "HF2",   pc900_pattern = "PC900|PC 900|PC-900" )

title: Extract per-camera model (
text: image_report Data frame least keys model column. Typically WildTrax image report. keys Character vector columns identify camera. Default: c("project","location"). prefer per-location_id, include (e.g., c("project","location","location_id")). model_col Column name (string bare) containing raw model text. Default "equipment_model". hf2_pattern Regex used detect HF2 models (case-insensitive). Default "HF2". pc900_pattern Regex used detect PC900 models (case-insensitive). Default "PC900|PC 900|PC-900".

title: Extract per-camera model (
text: Tibble keys + model (values: "hf2" "pc900").

title: Extract per-camera model (
text: Marcus Becker

title: Extract per-camera model (
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # image_reports is a WildTrax image report tibble model_lookup <- cam_extract_model_lookup(   image_reports,   keys      = c("project", "location"),   model_col = "equipment_model" ) } # }

what: Get a dataframe of operational days for each camera — cam_get_op_days
title: Get a dataframe of operational days for each camera — cam_get_op_days
text: Build per-day calendar camera deployment mark date operational -range image day. -range defined field--view flag: image_fov != "OOR" -range; "OOR" --range. default, NA image_fov treated -range.

title: Get a dataframe of operational days for each camera — cam_get_op_days
code: cam_get_op_days(   df,   grouping = c("project", "project_id", "location", "location_id"),   datetime_col = "image_date_time",   fov_col = "image_fov",   oor_flag = "OOR",   na_fov_means_inrange = TRUE,   trigger_col = "image_trigger_mode",   trigger_exclude_value = "CodeLoc Not Entered",   span = c("data", "operational"),   missing_as = TRUE )

title: Get a dataframe of operational days for each camera — cam_get_op_days
text: df Image report tibble/data.frame. grouping Camera identity columns (present used). datetime_col Timestamp column name. Default "image_date_time". fov_col Field--view flag column. Default "image_fov". oor_flag String indicating --range fov_col. Default "OOR". na_fov_means_inrange Logical; treat NA fov_col -range? Default TRUE. trigger_col Column indicates trigger mode. Default "image_trigger_mode". trigger_exclude_value Value trigger_col exclude entirely calendar ops logic. Default "CodeLoc Entered". span "data" "operational". Default "data". missing_as Fill days images expansion: one TRUE, FALSE, NA.

title: Get a dataframe of operational days for each camera — cam_get_op_days
text: Tibble grouping, date (Date), operating (logical/NA).

title: Get a dataframe of operational days for each camera — cam_get_op_days
text: function: Pre-filters rows trigger_col == trigger_exclude_value (default "CodeLoc Entered"). rows contribute operating days calendar span. Parses image timestamp Date. Flags -range fov_col != oor_flag (NA treated na_fov_means_inrange). Aggregates daily operating = (-range). Expands full daily calendar, filling missing days missing_as. span = "data" uses min..max date filtered images; span = "operational" trims first..last TRUE operating.

title: Get a dataframe of operational days for each camera — cam_get_op_days
text: Marcus Becker

title: Get a dataframe of operational days for each camera — cam_get_op_days
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # image_reports is a WildTrax image report tibble cal <- cam_get_op_days(   image_reports,   grouping   = c("project_id", "project", "location_id", "location"),   span       = "data",   missing_as = TRUE ) } # }

title: Identify
text: Flags boundaries animal image followed (one rows NONE) another animal image within short time window. split separate series intervening NONE indicates animal left FOV, even total animal--animal image gap small.

title: Identify
code: cam_obtain_n_gap_class(   cons_main_report,   grouping = c("project", "project_id", "location", "location_id"),   datetime_col = "image_date_time",   species_col = "species_common_name",   none_label = "NONE",   max_bridge_secs = 120,   require_same_species = TRUE )

title: Identify
text: cons_main_report Main report consolidated tags (e.g., cam_consolidate_tags()), requiring: image_date_time, image_id, species_common_name, camera identity columns. grouping Character vector camera identity columns. present used. Default: c("project","project_id","location","location_id"). datetime_col Name timestamp column. Default "image_date_time". species_col Name species column.   Default "species_common_name". none_label Label denoting NONE image. Default "NONE". max_bridge_secs Numeric, maximum animal--animal elapsed seconds still consider bridged series. Default 120. require_same_species Logical, require species /NONE block? Default TRUE.

title: Identify
text: tibble one row per detected boundary: grouping columns, image_id, image_date_time, species_common_name, gap_class = "N". none found, returns empty tibble warns.

title: Identify
text: Marcus Becker

what: Summarise total time by project, location, species, and season (with op-days) — cam_sum_total_time
title: Summarise total time by project, location, species, and season (with op-days) — cam_sum_total_time
text: Rolls image-series time (seconds) seasonal totals per deployment × species attaches number operating days per season. Seasons assigned Julian cutoffs provide (e.g., spring = 99, summer = 143, winter = 288). Output ready feed density calculations require total_duration total_season_days.

title: Summarise total time by project, location, species, and season (with op-days) — cam_sum_total_time
code: cam_sum_total_time(   series_df,   season_cutoffs = c(spring = 99L, summer = 143L, winter = 288L),   tz = Sys.timezone(),   op_days_df,   species_universe = NULL )

title: Summarise total time by project, location, species, and season (with op-days) — cam_sum_total_time
text: series_df Output cam_calc_time_by_series(); needs project, location, species_common_name, series_start, series_total_time season_cutoffs Named integer vector Julian cutoffs, e.g. c(spring=99L, summer=143L, winter=288L). Labels come names (lower-case recommended consistency EDD lookups). Cutoffs auto-sorted, order matter. tz Time zone used extract Julian day series_start. Defaults Sys.timezone() (local system timezone). WildTrax timestamps typically stored local time, default usually correct. Override explicitly (e.g. "America/Edmonton") running machine whose timezone match study area. op_days_df Operating-days table (required) zero-fill + days per season. Accepts EITHER: long: project, location, season, total_season_days (operating_days), wide: project, location, one column per season label (values day counts). Typically output cam_summarise_op_by_season(wide = TRUE). species_universe Optional vector used zero-fill across species

title: Summarise total time by project, location, species, and season (with op-days) — cam_sum_total_time
text: Tibble : project, location, species_common_name, season, total_duration, total_season_days

title: Summarise total time by project, location, species, and season (with op-days) — cam_sum_total_time
text: Season assignment series row assigned season using Julian day series_start chosen tz. Cutoffs must strictly increasing; season labels come names season_cutoffs used downstream (keep lower-case consistency EDD lookups). Aggregation Within project × location × species_common_name × season, function sums series_total_time produce total_duration seconds. Operating days op_days_df can supplied either long form (season column day-count column named total_season_days operating_days) wide form (one column per season label). function normalizes long table merges add total_season_days. Zero-filling ensure locations detected series still appear (0 duration), function constructs full grid cameras (op_days_df) × seasons, crossed species universe. species_universe supplied, uses species present series_df. guarantees downstream density steps see explicit zeros rather missing rows. Input requirements series_df must include: project, location, species_common_name, series_start (POSIXct parseable), series_total_time (seconds). op_days_df must provide operating-day counts per camera × season (long wide). Output tibble columns: project, location, species_common_name, season, total_duration (seconds), total_season_days (days).

title: Summarise total time by project, location, species, and season (with op-days) — cam_sum_total_time
text: Marcus Becker

title: Summarise total time by project, location, species, and season (with op-days) — cam_sum_total_time
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # series is the output of cam_calc_time_by_series() # op_days is the output of cam_summarise_op_by_season(wide = TRUE) dur <- cam_sum_total_time(   series_df        = series,   season_cutoffs   = c(spring = 99L, summer = 143L, winter = 288L),   op_days_df       = op_days,   species_universe = c("White-tailed Deer", "Moose", "Black Bear") ) } # }

what: Summarise operational days by user-defined seasons — cam_summarise_op_by_season
title: Summarise operational days by user-defined seasons — cam_summarise_op_by_season
text: Takes output cam_get_op_days() sums number operational days per season. Seasons defined Julian day start cutoffs can 1..n segments wrap year.

title: Summarise operational days by user-defined seasons — cam_summarise_op_by_season
code: cam_summarise_op_by_season(   calendar_df,   grouping = c("project", "project_id", "location", "location_id"),   seasons = c(spring = 99, summer = 143, winter = 288),   labels = NULL,   date_col = "date",   operating_col = "operating",   na_as = FALSE,   by_year = FALSE,   wide = TRUE )

title: Summarise operational days by user-defined seasons — cam_summarise_op_by_season
text: calendar_df tibble/data.frame least date (Date) operating (logical/NA), typically output cam_get_op_days(). grouping Character vector grouping columns keep summary. Defaults c("project","project_id","location","location_id"); columns present used. none present, aggregation global. seasons Season starts. Named numeric, unnamed numeric + labels, data frame season start columns. Defaults c(spring = 99, summer = 143, winter = 288). labels Optional labels seasons unnamed numeric vector. date_col Name date column calendar_df. Default "date". operating_col Name logical operating column. Default "operating". na_as Logical: treat NA operating TRUE counting? Default FALSE. by_year Logical: also summarise calendar year? Default FALSE. wide Logical: return one column per season (TRUE) long table season operating_days (FALSE). Default TRUE.

title: Summarise operational days by user-defined seasons — cam_summarise_op_by_season
text: tibble. wide mode: grouping cols (+ year by_year), one column per season (counts), total_days (row sum). long mode: grouping cols (+ year), season, operating_days, total_days.

title: Summarise operational days by user-defined seasons — cam_summarise_op_by_season
text: Season definitions (seasons) can supplied forms: Named numeric vector (recommended): e.g., c(spring = 99, summer = 143, winter = 288). Unnamed numeric + labels=: e.g., seasons = c(99, 143, 288), labels = c("spring","summer","winter"). Data frame columns season (labels) start (Julian day). Rules: Starts must integers 1..366, unique, sorted. Classification circular: last season runs day 366, wraps first start. Grouping: default function looks c("project","project_id","location", "location_id) use ones actually present calendar_df. none present, result aggregated across records. Operating NA handling: default operating = NA counted FALSE (na_as = FALSE). Set na_as = TRUE count unknown days operational.

title: Summarise operational days by user-defined seasons — cam_summarise_op_by_season
text: Marcus Becker

title: Summarise operational days by user-defined seasons — cam_summarise_op_by_season
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cal <- cam_get_op_days(image_reports,                        grouping = c("project_id","location","location_id"),                        span = "operational", missing_as = FALSE)  # Default seasons, by year, wide format sum1 <- cam_summarise_op_by_season(cal, by_year = TRUE, wide = TRUE)  # Two-season example, long format sum2 <- cam_summarise_op_by_season(   calendar_df = cal,   seasons     = c(IceFree = 120, FreezeUp = 305),   by_year     = FALSE,   wide        = FALSE ) } # }

what: Assign season labels to a vector of Julian days — .assign_season
title: Assign season labels to a vector of Julian days — .assign_season
text: Assign season labels vector Julian days

title: Assign season labels to a vector of Julian days — .assign_season
code: .assign_season(yday_vec, cuts, labs)

title: Assign season labels to a vector of Julian days — .assign_season
text: yday_vec Integer numeric vector Julian days (1–366). cuts Sorted integer vector season-start Julian days (one per season). labs Character vector season labels, length order cuts.

title: Assign season labels to a vector of Julian days — .assign_season
text: ordered factor season labels levels = labs.

title: Assign season labels to a vector of Julian days — .assign_season
text: Classification left-closed: day equal cutoff assigned season (e.g., Julian day 143 first day summer cuts = c(99, 143, 288)). Days fall first cutoff wrap last season, allowing year-crossing seasons (e.g., winter spanning late-year early-year).

title: Assign season labels to a vector of Julian days — .assign_season
code: cuts <- c(99L, 143L, 288L) labs <- c("spring", "summer", "winter") sciCentRverse:::.assign_season(c(98, 99, 142, 143, 287, 288, 365), cuts, labs) #> [1] winter spring spring summer summer winter winter #> Levels: spring < summer < winter

title: Estimate Total Processing Time — estimate_processing_time

title: Estimate Total Processing Time — estimate_processing_time
code: estimate_processing_time(n_features, nsub, end_time, start_time)

title: Estimate Total Processing Time — estimate_processing_time
text: n_features Numeric. total number features dataset. nsub Numeric. number features actually processed partial subset. end_time POSIXct. time subset processing ended. start_time POSIXct. time subset processing started.

title: Estimate Total Processing Time — estimate_processing_time
text: Character string. human-readable duration representing estimated total processing time features.

title: Estimate Total Processing Time — estimate_processing_time
code: # Example usage of the function  # Simulate a short processing run start_time <- Sys.time() Sys.sleep(2) # stand-in for some real processing end_time <- Sys.time()  # Estimate total time if the above was a test on 100 out of # 10,000 features estimate_processing_time(10000, 100, end_time, start_time) #> [1] "0 days, 0 hours, 3 minutes, 20 seconds"

what: Extract Raster Values by Year — extract_by_year

title: Extract Raster Values by Year — extract_by_year
code: extract_by_year(sf_object, raster_list, extract_args = list())

title: Extract Raster Values by Year — extract_by_year
text: sf_object sf, spatial features object year column. raster_list list, named list raster objects, names contain years format name_name_year. extract_args list, optional arguments passed terra::extract function (e.g., exact, ID).

title: Extract Raster Values by Year — extract_by_year
text: data.frame, data frame containing original columns band columns extracted raster values.

title: Extract Raster Values by Year — extract_by_year
code: # Example data sf_object <- sf::st_as_sf(data.frame(     site = c("A", "B"),     year = c(2020, 2021),     longitude = c(-114.07, -113.49),     latitude = c(51.05, 53.55) ), coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326)  raster_list <- list(     "data_2020" = terra::rast(array(1:30, dim = c(5, 6, 3)),         extent = c(-120, -110, 50, 55)     ),     "data_2021" = terra::rast(array(31:60, dim = c(5, 6, 3)),         extent = c(-120, -110, 50, 55)     ) ) names(raster_list[["data_2020"]]) <- c("Band1", "Band2", "Band3") names(raster_list[["data_2021"]]) <- c("Band1", "Band2", "Band3")  # Example function call extracted_values <- extract_by_year(     sf_object, raster_list,     extract_args = list(fun = "mean") )  # Example result printing print(extracted_values) #>   site year Band1 Band2 Band3 #> 1    A 2020    19    19    19 #> 2    B 2021    47    47    47

title: Run a Pipeline Step with Logging — run_step

code: set.seed(123) df <- data.frame(     block_id = rep(letters[1:4], each = 1000),     value = rnorm(4000) ) df_sub <- sample_blocks(     df,     frac = 0.05, block_column = "block_id" ) dplyr::count(df_sub, block_id) #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   block_id     n #>   <chr>    <int> #> 1 a           50 #> 2 b           50 #> 3 c           50 #> 4 d           50

what: sciCentRverse: Personal R Utility Functions — sciCentRverse-package
title: sciCentRverse: Personal R Utility Functions — sciCentRverse-package
text: internal R package prototyping, testing, refining reusable functions data processing, visualization, species distribution modelling, general analyses across Science Centre projects. Includes functions spatial data handling, raster extraction, time estimation, file loading, data formatting. internal R package prototyping, testing, refining reusable functions data processing, visualization, species distribution modelling, general analyses across Science Centre projects.

title: sciCentRverse: Personal R Utility Functions — sciCentRverse-package
text: add_alberta_flag(): Flag points inside Alberta using sf rnaturalearth. extract_by_year(): Extract data grouped year. read_tifs_to_list(), read_tifs_to_multiband(): Read rasters. estimate_processing_time(): Estimate runtime. load_most_recent_data(), load_rdata_files(): File loading helpers. sample_blocks(): Sampling helpers spatial workflows. set_terra_options(): Configure terra. snake_case(): String case utilities. style_active_file(): Format active file styler. summarize_column_classes(): Inspect data frame column classes.

title: sciCentRverse: Personal R Utility Functions — sciCentRverse-package
text: Maintainer: Brendan Casey bgcasey@ualberta.ca Authors: Marcus Becker mabecker@ualberta.ca

what: Set Terra Options with Buffer — set_terra_options
title: Set Terra Options with Buffer — set_terra_options
text: Configure terra options control memory usage, core allocation, temporary file storage. function calculates resource allocations based total system memory, number cores, resource percentage, optional memory buffer spikes. sets memfrac, memmax, tempdir options terra.

title: Set Terra Options with Buffer — set_terra_options
code: set_terra_options(   total_memory_gb,   total_cores,   resource_percent,   priority = "default",   buffer_percent = 10,   tempdir = tempdir() )

title: Set Terra Options with Buffer — set_terra_options
text: total_memory_gb Numeric, total system memory GB. total_cores Integer, total number cores available. resource_percent Numeric, percentage system resources allocate process. priority Character, resource allocation strategy: "memory", "speed", "default". Default "default". buffer_percent Numeric, additional percentage memory reserved buffer handle spikes. Default 10. tempdir Character, directory storing temporary files. Default tempdir().

title: Set Terra Options with Buffer — set_terra_options
text: Integer, number cores used parallel processing.

title: Set Terra Options with Buffer — set_terra_options
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cores <- set_terra_options(     total_memory_gb = 128,     total_cores = 20,     resource_percent = 50,     priority = "default",     buffer_percent = 10,     tempdir = "D:/r_temp/RtmpWSPfnw" ) print(cores) } # }

what: Convert Strings to Snake Case — snake_case
title: Convert Strings to Snake Case — snake_case
text: Converts vector strings snake_case inserting underscores words converting characters lowercase. also replaces spaces underscores.

title: Convert Strings to Snake Case — snake_case
code: snake_case(x)

title: Convert Strings to Snake Case — snake_case
text: x Character vector. input strings converted.

title: Convert Strings to Snake Case — snake_case
text: character vector converted strings snake_case.

title: Convert Strings to Snake Case — snake_case
code: snake_case(c("Normal 1991_2020 AHM", "Normal 1991_2020 bFFP")) #> [1] "normal_1991_2020_ahm"   "normal_1991_2020_b_ffp" # Returns: c("normal_1991_2020_ahm", "normal_1991_2020_b_ffp")

what: Style the Active R File — style_active_file
title: Style the Active R File — style_active_file
text: Locate currently active R source file (RStudio –file command line argument) run styler::style_file using tidyverse style 2-space indent.

title: Style the Active R File — style_active_file
code: style_active_file()

title: Style the Active R File — style_active_file
text: Invisibly returns path styled file, NULL active file found.

what: Generate a Data Frame of Column Classes — summarize_column_classes
title: Generate a Data Frame of Column Classes — summarize_column_classes
text: function takes list data frames returns summary data frame showing classes column across data frames. Missing columns data frames represented NA.

title: Generate a Data Frame of Column Classes — summarize_column_classes
code: summarize_column_classes(all_data)

title: Generate a Data Frame of Column Classes — summarize_column_classes
text: all_data List. list data frames.

title: Generate a Data Frame of Column Classes — summarize_column_classes
text: data frame rows representing data frames columns representing column names data frames. values classes columns NA column missing data frame.

title: Generate a Data Frame of Column Classes — summarize_column_classes
code: # Example usage: all_data <- list(     df1 = data.frame(a = 1:3, b = letters[1:3]),     df2 = data.frame(a = 4:6, c = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE)) ) column_classes_df <- summarize_column_classes(all_data) print(column_classes_df) #>   data_frame       a         b       c #> 1        df1 integer character    <NA> #> 2        df2 integer      <NA> logical

what: Minimal Theme for Scientific Plots — theme_science
title: Minimal Theme for Scientific Plots — theme_science
text: function creates minimalistic ggplot2 theme scientific plots ggplot2. removes gridlines, simplifies axis formatting, adds clean, bold titles labels.

title: Minimal Theme for Scientific Plots — theme_science
code: theme_science()

title: Minimal Theme for Scientific Plots — theme_science
text: ggplot2 theme object.

title: Minimal Theme for Scientific Plots — theme_science
code: # Example usage: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +     geom_point() +     labs(title = "Example Plot", x = "Weight", y = "mpg") +     theme_science() print(p)

code: # Example usage: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +     geom_point() +     labs(title = "Example Plot", x = "Weight", y = "mpg") +     theme_science() print(p)
